jobs.title: F&B Service Manager
jobs.description:
Your Purpose

The F&B Service Manager is a strategic headquarters-based role responsible for providing leadership, oversight, and guidance for all Food & Beverage service operations across the fleet. This role ensures service excellence, consistency in guest experience, and alignment with corporate standards. Acting as the primary liaison between shipboard teams and corporate leadership, the Manager of F&B Service drives quality, innovation, compliance, and talent development across all F&B operations. This role reports directly to the Vice President of F&B.

Your Impact

Strategic Operations & Standards

- Define, implement, and maintain fleet-wide F&B service standards and best practices.

- Develop and oversee strategic initiatives to enhance guest experience and operational efficiency across all vessels.

- Coordinate with shipboard teams to ensure compliance with corporate policies, procedures, and brand standards.

- Conduct remote audits, operational reviews, and assessments to maintain service excellence.

Guest Experience & Quality Assurance

- Monitor guest satisfaction and F&B performance metrics, analyze trends, and recommend improvements.

- Collaborate with culinary and beverage teams to innovate and elevate the onboard F&B offering.

- Ensure alignment with global health, safety, and hygiene protocols through corporate-led programs.

Training & Leadership Development

- Design and oversee training programs for F&B teams to ensure consistency with the brand’s service philosophy.

- Mentor and develop shipboard F&B leaders to support career progression and succession planning.

Human Resources & Leadership Oversight

- Collaborate with HR on performance reviews, promotions, rotations, and career pathing for F&B personnel.

- Promote a high-performance culture rooted in recognition, development, and fairness.

Fleet Support & Headquarters Coordination

- Serve as the principal corporate liaison for all F&B service matters across the fleet.

- Coordinate F&B equipment planning, material sourcing, and integration of brand standards.

- Compile and analyze operational reports, voyage summaries, and performance metrics for corporate leadership.

- Participate in key shipboard meetings remotely or in person as required.

Financial Oversight

- Support financial sustainability and profitability of F&B operations across the fleet.

- Implement continuous improvement programs to achieve operational and financial targets.

- Oversee cost control, inventory planning, and resource allocation in coordination with shipboard teams.

Your Journey so far

- Minimum 15 years in high-end Food & Beverage operations.

- At least 5 years in senior leadership roles within luxury hospitality or cruise operations.

- Proven experience in multi-site operational oversight.

Your Essentials

Leadership & Communication

- Strategic, dynamic leader with experience managing multicultural teams.

- Excellent communication skills, fluent in English; additional languages an asset.

Technical & Operational Knowledge

- Proficient in Microsoft Office and industry-specific systems.

- Strong understanding of HR protocols, labor regulations, and compliance standards.

- Expert knowledge of F&B operations, beverage programming, and luxury hospitality standards.

Professional Competencies

- Strategic thinker with operational agility and strong business acumen.

- Results-driven, detail-oriented, and highly collaborative.

- Capable of working cross-functionally across corporate and shipboard teams.

Our commitment

We are committed to building a future that values diverse perspectives, embraces the world beyond borders, and fosters an inclusive environment where every individual feels valued, respected and empowered to be their authentic selves. Our commitment extends to taking meaningful, measurable actions that have a long-term positive impact on our guests, our employees and our planet.

Ready to turn your passion into something extraordinary? Join us at MSC Cruises, where new opportunities await. Apply today to be part of a global team that is pushing boundaries and achieving something remarkable. Your journey starts here!

jobs.title: Shore Excursions Product Development Manager
jobs.description:
Your Purpose

In line with current and future tourism trends, the Shore Excursions Product Development Manager oversees product content and delivery, negotiates costs, and develops new tours to grow revenue and enhance guest satisfaction in specific geographical regions.

Your Impact

- Lead the development of innovative shore excursion products and manage the tour program for assigned regions.

- Source and procure high-quality excursions in line with seasonal demand and company standards.

- Monitor market trends, guest preferences, and potential risks to develop effective action and improvement plans.

- Analyze guest feedback and satisfaction scores to drive continuous product and service enhancements.

- Build and maintain relationships with up to 100 tour operators, overseeing a portfolio of 1,000+ tours.

- Negotiate contracts with suppliers, manage large-scale bids, and optimize costs to ensure profitability and efficiency.

- Collaborate cross-functionally with Legal, Insurance, and Operations teams to ensure compliance and risk mitigation.

- Oversee product creation, feedback analysis, and product revamping to maintain competitive and engaging offerings.

- Ensure inventory accuracy and perform regular site inspections within assigned destinations.

- Represent the company at government meetings, industry events, and travel conventions to strengthen partnerships and brand presence.

Your Journey so far

- 10 years of experience in the Shore Excursions Department within the cruise industry (either onboard or ashore) is preferred. Experience in shore excursion operations within the hospitality industry or with a global tour operator may also be considered.

- 5–10+ years of proven experience in global shore excursion operations is required, including product development, bid management, and cross-functional collaboration with legal, insurance, and operations stakeholders.

- A bachelor’s degree in tourism and proficiency in multiple languages are highly preferred.

Your Essentials

- Flexibility and strong analytical skills.

- Excellent communication and collaboration abilities.

- IT-savvy; experience with Fidelio is highly advantageous.

- Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office applications (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Publisher).

- Extensive knowledge of shore excursion products and operational delivery.

- Fluent in English with a good command of Italian; additional languages are an asset.

- Willingness to travel up to 25% of the time.

- Must hold an EU passport or have the legal right to work in Switzerland.

Our commitment

We are committed to building a future that values diverse perspectives, embraces the world beyond borders, and fosters an inclusive environment where every individual feels valued, respected and empowered to be their authentic selves. Our commitment extends to taking meaningful, measurable actions that have a long-term positive impact on our guests, our employees and our planet.

Ready to turn your passion into something extraordinary? Join us at MSC Cruises, where new opportunities await. Apply today to be part of a global team that is pushing boundaries and achieving something remarkable. Your journey starts here!

jobs.title: Senior HR Business Partner

descriptionByLocale.it: Are you experienced in strategic HR management? Join us as a Senior HR Business Partner, where you will provide hands-on HR support and drive performance in a dynamic hospitality environment. Collaborate with senior leaders to translate business priorities into effective people solutions and make a significant impact on organizational effectiveness.
descriptionByLocale.en: Your Purpose Working at the heart of the business and in close partnership with the Corporate HR team, the Senior HR Business Partner provides strategic and hands-on HR support to assigned corporate functions and offices worldwide, within a fast-paced, service-driven hospitality environment. Reporting to the Head of HR, this role partners with senior leaders to translate business priorities into effective people solutions, while ensuring consistent application of Group HR policies, processes, and standards. The Senior HRBP plays a key role in driving performance, engagement, and organisational effectiveness in a complex, international organisation where operational excellence, agility, and customer focus are critical. Your Impact - Act as the primary HR partner for assigned corporate functions, providing strategic advice and operational HR support aligned with business objectives; - Equip leaders with the tools, frameworks, and guidance required to effectively manage their teams in line with Group values and people policies; - Partner with senior leaders to support workforce planning and organisational needs, ensuring capabilities and capacity are aligned with current and future business requirements; - Collaborate closely with Talent Acquisition and hiring managers to define recruitment needs and support delivery of hiring plans in line with agreed KPIs; - Drive performance and accountability by supporting the implementation of the Group performance management framework, including goal setting, evaluation cycles, and manager capability building; - Support talent management processes within the business perimeter, including talent reviews, identification of high-potential employees, succession discussions, and development actions, in coordination with the Head of HR and relevant Centers of Expertise; - Advise managers on employee relations matters, ensuring fair, consistent, and compliant people decisions; - Support change management and organisational initiatives by assessing people impact, supporting leaders through transitions, and ensuring clear and effective communication; - Contribute to key HR cycles such as annual compensation reviews, talent reviews, and employee engagement initiatives; - Work closely with HR Operations, Payroll, and HRIS teams to ensure smooth execution of HR processes and digital tools; - Participate in global HR projects and initiatives, contributing business insight and supporting effective local implementation; - Conduct exit interviews, analyse turnover trends, and contribute to retention and engagement actions within the business perimeter. Your Journey so far - Master’s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related field; - Minimum 10 years of progressive HR experience in a matrixed, multinational environment, including at least 5 -7 years in a Senior HR Business Partner or equivalent role; - Proven HR experience within the hospitality, cruise, travel or customer-centric industry, with a strong understanding of service culture; - Demonstrated experience partnering with senior leaders in a corporate or headquarters environment; - Strong project management skills, with the ability to plan, coordinate, and deliver HR initiatives involving multiple stakeholders and tight timelines; - Solid exposure to performance management, talent processes, employee relations, and organisational support; - Experience working with global HR policies, frameworks, and HR systems (HRIS); - Knowledge of Swiss Labour Law is an advantage; - Fluent in English; French is an advantage. Your Essentials - Strong business acumen with the ability to translate strategy into pragmatic HR solutions; - Credible and confident advisor, able to influence, challenge, and build trust with senior stakeholders; - Strong understanding of hospitality and service excellence cultures, with the ability to balance people care, performance, and operational demands; - Ability to balance strategic thinking with hands-on delivery in a fast-paced, high-expectation environment. - Structured, reliable, and comfortable operating across multiple priorities; - Collaborative mindset, with the ability to work effectively across HR functions and with diverse stakeholders; - High standards of integrity, discretion, and professional judgment; - Flexible, resilient, and solution-oriented, with a continuous improvement mindset. Our commitment We are committed to building a future that values diverse perspectives, embraces the world beyond borders, and fosters an inclusive environment where every individual feels valued, respected and empowered to be their authentic selves. Our commitment extends to taking meaningful, measurable actions that have a long-term positive impact on our guests, our employees and our planet. Ready to turn your passion into something extraordinary? Join us at MSC Cruises, where new opportunities await. Apply today to be part of a global team that is pushing boundaries and achieving something remarkable. Your journey starts here!

jobs.title: Multimedia and AV Manager
jobs.description:
Your Purpose

Lead and define the strategic planning, deployment, and operational management of the Multimedia Department while supporting audiovisual (AV) systems across fleet and corporate infrastructure. Oversee fleet multimedia content, technology innovation, and AV operations to maximise guest experience and maintain consistent brand standards. Working closely with global teams, new build projects, and external stakeholders, drive collaboration across Entertainment, Marketing, IT, Digital, Production, and technical functions to deliver seamless digital experiences. The AV scope expands beyond traditional multimedia, supporting wider technical initiatives across the Entertainment division — including theatrical and guest activities — and acting as a key bridge between operational entertainment needs and technical delivery teams.

Your Impact

- Manage multimedia and AV operations, including the design, implementation, optimisation, and lifecycle management of audio, video, broadcast, digital signage, and content delivery systems.

- Partner with shoreside and onboard technical teams to ensure equipment reliability, structured maintenance planning, operational scheduling, and agreed repair strategies across the fleet.

- Lead cross-functional initiatives with Entertainment, Marketing, IT, Digital, Production, and technical stakeholders to deliver innovative onboard and shoreside guest experiences.

- Act as the primary liaison between Entertainment, Production vendors, external contractors, and the Show and Multimedia department, translating creative and operational requirements into scalable technical solutions.

- Advise and support New Build projects on multimedia and AV technical requirements, ensuring future-proof, sustainable technology integration.

- Collaborate with Brand, Marketing, and content providers to develop, brief, and implement new ship-based media content strategies and onboard marketing initiatives.

- Define and project manage operational workflows and delivery plans for Entertainment and onboard marketing content across shipboard screens and digital platforms.

- Oversee vendor relationships, procurement activities, and associated budgets to maintain cost efficiency while ensuring technical excellence.

- Monitor and evaluate global technology trends to keep fleet systems innovative, competitive, and aligned with evolving guest expectations.

- Develop, maintain, and continuously improve standard operating procedures and technical standards, ensuring fleetwide consistency, safety, and operational efficiency in line with corporate Entertainment guidelines.

- Supervise, appraise, and mentor ship-based and touring technicians globally, ensuring compliance with safety, maritime, and corporate policies.

- Support live events, broadcasting, corporate communications, and multimedia initiatives through strategic planning combined with hands-on technical leadership.

- Manage and govern visual content provided by internal and external partners for broadcast across onboard platforms.

Your Journey so far

- Extensive experience within cruise environments is a must.

- Senior operational and managerial experience within the Entertainment industry.

- Mother tongue English; Italian, German, or Spanish advantageous.

- Strong background in broadcasting, web, TV, theatrical production, or digital media operations with managerial responsibility.

- Knowledge of enterprise Content Management Systems and digital platforms.

- Solid IT literacy with understanding of systems architecture and AV integration.

- Full understanding of LES systems within the maritime industry.

- Advanced computer literacy (Microsoft Office, Adobe Creative Suite, and CAD software).

- Strong project management and stakeholder coordination skills.

- Proven ability to work autonomously, solve complex operational challenges, and manage global teams.

- International mindset with experience supporting mixed-nationality audiences and global operations.

- Willingness to travel extensively.

Your Essentials

- SWISS Resident or Visa Holder

Our commitment

We are committed to building a future that values diverse perspectives, embraces the world beyond borders, and fosters an inclusive environment where every individual feels valued, respected and empowered to be their authentic selves. Our commitment extends to taking meaningful, measurable actions that have a long-term positive impact on our guests, our employees and our planet.

Ready to turn your passion into something extraordinary? Join us at MSC Cruises, where new opportunities await. Apply today to be part of a global team that is pushing boundaries and achieving something remarkable. Your journey starts here!

jobs.title: SPA Product Manager
jobs.description:
Your Purpose

The SPA Product Manager is responsible for the strategic development, management, and optimization of spa products, treatments, and retail offerings. This role ensures that all spa products align with brand positioning, guest expectations, and commercial objectives, while driving revenue, innovation, and operational excellence across the spa portfolio.

Your Impact

- Define and execute the spa product strategy, including treatments, retail products, and wellness concepts, in line with brand standards and business goals

- Manage the full product lifecycle, from concept development and supplier selection to launch, performance  monitoring, and optimization

- Collaborate with internal stakeholders (Spa Operations, Marketing, Training, Procurement, Finance) to ensure seamless product implementation

- Lead supplier relationships, and performance reviews.

- Analyze product performance through KPIs, guest feedback, and financial results, and implement datadriven improvements

- Ensure all products comply with regulatory, safety, and brand standards across all locations.

- Support the development of pricing strategies, promotions, and revenue-driving initiatives.

- Work closely with Training teams to ensure therapists and staff are fully trained on products and treatments.

- Monitor market trends, and innovations within the spa and wellness industry to maintain a competitive offering.

- Support new openings, refurbishments, and special projects related to spa product development.

Your Journey so far

- Strong commercial and analytical mindset with proven experience in product performance management

- Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills

- Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously in a fast-paced environment

- Strategic thinking combined with hands-on execution

- Strong negotiation and supplier management skills

- High attention to detail with a focus on quality and guest experience

- Proven experience in spa, wellness, beauty, or hospitality product management

- Strong understanding of spa operations, treatments, and retail environments

- Experience working with premium or luxury brands preferre

Your Essentials

- 10-15 years of experience in SPA and Wellness including Medispa treatments , retail products, and wellness concepts,

- Proven experience in managining the full product lifecycle, from concept development and supplier selection, legal requirements, launch, performance monitoring, and optimization.

- Excellent communication and leadership skills especially in guiding large teams and influencing stakeholders.

- Fluency in English is mandatory any other language is a plus.

- Strong leadership skills to oversee SPA operations across multiple locations.

- Experience with project management financial analysis and revenue management in the wellness or hospitality industry.

- Ability to work effectively with departments such as Marketing Guest Services and Revenue Management to create a cohesive wellness experience for guests.

- Swiss nationality or right to work in Switzerland.

Our commitment

We are committed to building a future that values diverse perspectives, embraces the world beyond borders, and fosters an inclusive environment where every individual feels valued, respected and empowered to be their authentic selves. Our commitment extends to taking meaningful, measurable actions that have a long-term positive impact on our guests, our employees and our planet.

Ready to turn your passion into something extraordinary? Join us at MSC Cruises, where new opportunities await. Apply today to be part of a global team that is pushing boundaries and achieving something remarkable. Your journey starts here!

jobs.title: Senior Project Manager - Infrastructure Investments (Caribbean)

titleByLocale.it: Senior Project Manager - Investimenti in Infrastrutture (Caribico)
titleByLocale.fr: Responsable de Projet Senior - Investissements en Infrastructures (Caraïbes)

jobs.description:
Your Purpose

We are seeking a high-caliber, dedicated PMO Lead / Senior Project Manager to oversee a strategic infrastructure investment project in the Caribbean, while establishing governance and execution discipline across current and future projects in the investment pipeline.
This role carries full accountability for project delivery, spanning finance, construction, commercial, operational, HR, regulatory, and stakeholder dimensions. The successful candidate will act as the single point of coordination and control between investors, contractors, operators, lenders, and government stakeholders.

Your Impact

• Establish and lead a Project Management System, including governance, reporting, controls, and KPIs.
• Drive end-to-end execution of major infrastructure projects, from development through construction and into operations.
• Oversees construction delivery and project financials, including budgets, cash flow, CAPEX/OPEX tracking, lender requirements, and reporting.
• Lead commercial and contract management, supplier, and concession PPP agreements.
• Ensure operational readiness and handover, including systems, staffing, and procedures.
• Coordinate HR and workforce planning, ensuring compliance with local labor laws and effective contractor management.
• Identify and manage project risks across technical, financial, regulatory, ESG, and political dimensions.
• Provide PMO oversight and execution support for additional projects in the development funnel.

Your Journey so far

• Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Finance, Business, or related field; Master’s degree preferred.
• 15+ years of experience in large-scale infrastructure or real asset projects.
• Demonstrated leadership of complex, capital-intensive projects with multiple stakeholders.
• Strong experience across construction, project finance, commercial contracts, and operations.
• Experience in emerging markets and/or the Caribbean region strongly preferred.
• PMP / PRINCE2 or equivalent certification preferred.

Your Essentials

• Hands-on, structured, and highly accountable
• Executive presence and credibility with senior stakeholders
• Comfortable working in complex, multi-jurisdictional environments
• Willing to travel and be on-site as required

Our commitment

We are committed to building a future that values diverse perspectives, embraces the world beyond borders, and fosters an inclusive environment where every individual feels valued, respected and empowered to be their authentic selves. Our commitment extends to taking meaningful, measurable actions that have a long-term positive impact on our guests, our employees and our planet.

jobs.title: Director of Operations - Hospitality Infrastructure
jobs.description:
Your Purpose

The Director of Operations is accountable for the design, mobilization, opening and ongoing operation of large-scale cruise and hospitality infrastructure assets.
The role ensures continuity from development through steady-state operations, acting as the operational owner from early construction phases through long-term performance.
Working in parallel with Development, Construction and Guest Experience, the Director of Operations guarantees that assets are operable by design, fully ready at opening, and sustainably efficient, safe and profitable thereafter.

Your Impact

1. Operational Leadership Across the Asset Lifecycle
Own end-to-end operational responsibility from pre-opening through live operations.
Define the long-term operating vision, KPIs and service standards.
Ensure continuity of design, culture and systems from project phase into steady-state operations.

2. Pre-Opening & Mobilization (Parallel to Construction)
Lead the operational readiness program aligned with construction milestones.
In coordination with Corporate Guest Services (F&B, HK, Entertainment etc.), define Procurements needs and implement it. FFE/OWS. 
Influence design decisions to ensure operational efficiency, safety and maintainability.
Establish operating structures, SOPs and governance well ahead of opening.
Manage handovers from construction to operations, including phased turnovers.
For each project, identify personnel need and -in conjunction with HR- ensure the operational ability of new assets. Establish Training Programs for operational readiness.

3. Post-Opening Operations Management
Direct daily operations across all departments (hospitality, F&B, retail, attractions, marine, security, maintenance, guest services).
Ensure delivery of guest experience, safety, service quality and brand standards.
Drive operational stability during ramp-up and maturity phases.
Lead continuous improvement initiatives across the asset.

4. Workforce, Training & Culture
Build, lead and retain a high-performing, multi-national workforce.
Maintain and evolve training programs post-opening.
Embed a safety-first, service-oriented and performance-driven culture.

5. Financial & Commercial Performance
Own operational P&L, cost controls and productivity metrics.
Manage operating budgets, capex planning and lifecycle reserves.
Collaborate with commercial teams to optimize revenue (F&B, retail, experiences, shore excursions where applicable).

6. Compliance, Safety & Risk Management
Ensure ongoing compliance with all regulatory, maritime, health, safety and environmental standards.
Lead emergency preparedness, crisis management and business continuity planning.
Serve as the accountable executive for audits and inspections.

Your Journey so far

- 15+ years senior operations leadership in hospitality, cruise destinations, resorts, ports, theme parks or large hospitality assets.

- Demonstrated ownership of both pre-opening and long-term operations.

- Strong background working alongside development, construction and technical services teams.

- Experience in Caribbean or island environments strongly preferred.

Your Essentials

- Operational readiness & lifecycle operations

- Large-scale workforce leadership

- Financial & P&L accountability

- Asset lifecycle & maintenance management

- Multi-operator and concession management

Our commitment

We are committed to building a future that values diverse perspectives, embraces the world beyond borders, and fosters an inclusive environment where every individual feels valued, respected and empowered to be their authentic selves. Our commitment extends to taking meaningful, measurable actions that have a long-term positive impact on our guests, our employees and our planet.

Ready to turn your passion into something extraordinary? Join us at MSC Cruises, where new opportunities await. Apply today to be part of a global team that is pushing boundaries and achieving something remarkable. Your journey starts here!

jobs.title: Digital Acquisition Manager
jobs.description:
Your Purpose

The Senior Digital Acquisition Manager will be responsible for leading the digital marketing strategy across all MSC Cruises markets, with a focus on driving direct sales through digital advertising levers, setting and achieving KPIs, and developing innovative digital campaigns across multiple channels. The successful candidate will have a proven track record in performance marketing and digital sales development at a positive ROAS, along with strong expertise in digital advertising campaign strategy, paid media planning and implementation, and team leadership. Additionally, the role will involve developing affiliation and partnership programs as part of an integrated digital engagement strategy to broaden reach, drive qualified traffic, and support long-term customer value.

Your Impact

• Develop and maintain strong relationships with MSC stakeholders across various markets, with a deep understanding of their business needs, challenges, and opportunities—while balancing the global objectives set by headquarters.

• Set and achieve KPIs for online sales through digital advertising levers, ensuring the media agency delivers against targets and contractual obligations.

• Lead internal meetings, QBRs, and annual reviews by providing strategic insights and recommendations based on campaign performance, website analytics, market trends, and the competitive landscape.

• Plan and implement innovative performance marketing campaigns across Paid Search, Display, Social, Native, Digital Audio, and Video channels—tailored to business goals and market dynamics.

• Collaborate with internal teams—including Creative, Social Media, Data & Analytics, SEO, Content, Product, and agency partners—to ensure the timely delivery of high-quality assets aligned with annual digital sales targets.

• Identify and pursue opportunities to enhance revenue streams through existing and new digital strategies, while improving campaign ROAS and experimenting with innovations in the adtech and martech ecosystem.

• Develop and manage lead generation strategies, ensuring effective customer acquisition flows and nurturing tactics are in place across all paid media touchpoints.

• Maintain oversight of account resourcing and profitability to ensure HQ and local markets are managed efficiently and profitably.

• Serve as the main escalation point for concerns raised by internal teams or local market stakeholders.

• Continuously review and optimize operational workflows, proposing and implementing process improvements where relevant.

• Collaborate with country and HQ stakeholders to prepare and manage the annual digital marketing budget, aligned with MSC Cruises’ B2C revenue goals.

• Lead and optimize affiliate and partnership programs to drive qualified traffic and conversions, ensuring alignment with digital acquisition strategy and performance goals.

• Manage key partners and co-branded initiatives, coordinating with internal teams to ensure seamless execution and tracking.
 

Your Journey so far

• 7+ years of experience in a similar role.
• Hands-on experience with analytics and ad server tools such as GA4, Adobe Analytics, Campaign Manager and their relevant reporting frameworks.
• Proven experience in managing, mentoring, and upskilling team members.
• Past experience in lead generation strategy and execution is a strong plus.
• Solid experience in budget ownership and cost control.

Your Essentials

• Fluent in English (additional languages are a plus).
• Strong analytical skills and the ability to leverage data for informed decision-making.
• Proven track record of driving direct sales growth and improving ROAS through high-performing digital advertising campaigns.
• Passion for digital marketing, performance media, and innovative digital solutions.
• Deep knowledge of Google and Meta ad platforms, including setup auditing, implementation, and performance analysis with actionable insights.
• Excellent organizational, project management, and problem-solving skills.
• Proficient in Microsoft Office and data visualization tools such as Datorama.
• Strong collaboration and communication skills, with the ability to work effectively across cross-functional teams and external partners.
• Ability to identify trends in data and transform insights into actionable strategic recommendations.
• Self-starter with the ability to work independently and take initiative.
• Confident, articulate presenter—comfortable engaging with senior stakeholders and internal teams.
• Strong strategic thinking, capable of acting as a ‘Trusted Advisor’ to senior leadership.
• High attention to detail and ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced, dynamic environment.
• Positive attitude, proactive mindset, and a drive to embrace new challenges.
• Knowledge of affiliate platforms such as Awin, Kwanko, and similar tools. Strong network of industry contacts to initiate partnership opportunities and brand collaborations.

Our commitment

We are committed to building a future that values diverse perspectives, embraces the world beyond borders, and fosters an inclusive environment where every individual feels valued, respected and empowered to be their authentic selves. Our commitment extends to taking meaningful, measurable actions that have a long-term positive impact on our guests, our employees and our planet.

Ready to turn your passion into something extraordinary? Join us at MSC Cruises, where new opportunities await. Apply today to be part of a global team that is pushing boundaries and achieving something remarkable. Your journey starts here!

titleByLocale.en: Head of Learning, Development and Talent Management
titleByLocale.it: Responsabile Learning, Development and Talent Management
titleByLocale.de: Leiter Learning, Development and Talent Management
titleByLocale.fr: Responsable Learning, Development and Talent Management

jobs.description:
Your Purpose

The Head of Learning & Development (Onboard & Ashore) and Talent Management (Ashore) is responsible for defining and leading the global strategy for Learning and Development across both shipboard and shore-based employees, while overseeing Talent Management and Succession Planning for shore-based populations only.
The role ensures the company develops the capabilities required to deliver exceptional guest experiences, operational excellence, and strong leadership across a global workforce, including thousands of shipboard crew and a diverse shore-based organization.
For shipboard employees, the role focuses on building world-class operational and service capabilities through structured training, leadership development, and career progression frameworks that support fleet performance and guest satisfaction.
For shore-based employees, the role leads both Learning & Development and Talent Management, including leadership development, succession planning, and talent pipeline development for corporate and operational leadership roles.
Working closely with senior operational leaders across Operations, Guest Experience, and Corporate Functions, this role drives a culture of continuous learning, leadership excellence, and organizational capability building across the company’s global operations.

Your Impact

Global Learning & Development Strategy (Onboard & Ashore)

- Define and implement the global Learning & Development strategy covering both shipboard crew and shore-based employees.

- Develop an integrated learning ecosystem that supports operational excellence onboard and leadership capability ashore.

- Ensure learning initiatives directly support strategic priorities such as:

- guest experience excellence

- safety and compliance

- operational performance

- leadership effectiveness

- digital and business transformation

- Oversee onboarding and development frameworks to accelerate employee integration and performance across both shipboard and shore environments.

- Lead the adoption of modern learning approaches including digital learning, simulation-based training, microlearning, and blended learning solutions.

 

Shipboard Learning & Development

- Lead the design and delivery of the global shipboard learning and development strategy across the fleet.

- Partner closely with Marine Operations, Hotel Operations, Food & Beverage, and Guest Experience leadership to ensure training programs support operational priorities and service excellence.

- Ensure consistent training standards across the fleet while accommodating the operational realities of shipboard environments.

jobs.title: CRM Director
jobs.description:
Where passion meets opportunity

Discover your journey into the extraordinary.

Your Purpose

Explora Journeys is a vibrant, cosmopolitan, European luxury brand, imagined in the heart of Swiss hospitality in Geneva. Leveraging our parent company MSC Group’s hundreds of years of maritime expertise, our fleet of six luxury ships (four currently in the pipeline) are being built and designed in a totally different and unique way that will transform and redefine the cruise experience, creating a category of its own, appealing to the next generation of luxury travellers. 
The CRM Director – Insights, Customer Lifecycle & Marketing Automation leads the strategy and activation of data-driven customer engagement programs to inform the business and support commercial growth, loyalty and lifetime value.
The role acts as business lead for lifecycle marketing and marketing automation, working in close partnership with Commercial, Digital, IT, Data and Legal teams to ensure scalable, compliant and high-performing customer communications. He/ She leads the customer’s insights area recommending how to approach the brand evolution assessment. The role is based in Geneva, Switzerland.

Your Impact

1. Lifecycle Strategy & Co-Ownership
• Lead the definition of the global customer lifecycle strategy organizing the customer journey vision with Commercial, Digital and Guest Experience teams.
• Translate lifecycle ambition into structured activation roadmaps across owned marketing automation channels.
• Define lifecycle design principles, segmentation logic and engagement frameworks aligned with brand and commercial objectives.
• Ensure cross-functional alignment between customer experience, commercial priorities and lifecycle communications.

2. Journey Activation & Channel Governance
• Lead automated journey orchestration primarily across owned CRM channels 
• Establish governance for segmentation, personalization and campaign standards.
• Coordinate with other channel owners (e.g., Media, Social, Digital) to ensure journey consistency.
• Drive structured adoption of personalization and audience activation tools.

3. Platform Business Ownership 
• Act as business product owner of marketing automation platforms (e.g., Salesforce Marketing Cloud).
• Define functional requirements, prioritization and business roadmap.
• Partner with IT, as technical owner, for architecture, integrations, security and scalability.
• Ensure operational excellence of automations, data flows and activation processes.

4. Data Governance & Compliance
• Define lifecycle communication standards in alignment with Legal and IT.
• Ensure consent management and subscription governance frameworks are operationalized within marketing automation tools.
• Support privacy-by-design principles across activation use cases.

5. Commercial Impact & Performance
• Define CRM contribution metrics and dashboards.
• Monitor engagement, retention and revenue influence.
• Provide optimization roadmaps to senior leadership.

6. Lead the development of a customer insights framework, leveraging CRM data, external ad hoc research to inform brand health evolution. Translate customer understanding into actionable growth opportunities. Partner with Guest Experience teams, Marketing and Commercial teams to design and prioritize customer research initiatives, including satisfaction tracking, journey analysis, loyalty drivers, brand tracking,etc.

7. Team & Capability Building
• Build a high-performing marketing CRM team.
• Elevate internal understanding of marketing automation capabilities and limitations.
• Drive adoption and maturity of audience strategy and segmentation practices.

Your Journey so far

•    15+ years of experience in CRM, customer lifecycle management, marketing automation or customer data roles, ideally in global and local environments.
•    Proven experience driving customer acquisition, engagement, loyalty and retention through CRM at scale.
•    Proven experience designing and managing complex customer journeys across multiple channels, markets and touchpoints, including long consideration and purchase cycles
•    Deep understanding of CRM platforms, marketing automation tools and data-driven marketing, 
•    Solid knowledge on global standards for data capture, consent management, governance, and enrichment
•    Strong analytical mindset with experience translating CRM data into actionable insights. Experience leading customer insights initiatives such as segmentation studies, loyalty drivers, NPS/satisfaction research or journey diagnostics.
•     Proven people manager, able to build, structure and develop teams to deliver measurable business outcomes.
•     Strong track record of working hands-on while leading strategically, especially in complex or fast-growth environments.
•     Experience managing agencies and external partners.
•     Prior experience in luxury, premium or high-end consumer brands strongly preferred as well as Travel & hospitality

Your Essentials

• Strategic, customer-centric and commercially minded leader.
• Highly analytical, data-driven and comfortable translating insights into action.
• Technically savvy, with the ability to bridge business, data and technology teams.
• Structured, detail-oriented and able to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced, evolving environment.
• Outstanding collaboration and influencing skills, with proven ability to operate in a matrix organization.

At Explora Journeys, we are redefining luxury ocean travel — where discovery, well-being, and a deep respect for the seas come together in harmony. 
We seek passionate professionals who share our vision of a more immersive, transformative way to discover the world’s most inspiring destinations by sea.
Are you ready to turn your passion into something extraordinary? Join us at Explora Journeys, where an ocean of opportunities awaits. Your journey to the Ocean State of Mind starts here.

descriptionByLocale.it: Join our team as a CRM Director, where you will lead data-driven customer engagement programs and lifecycle marketing strategies. Leverage your expertise in CRM and marketing automation to drive commercial growth and enhance customer loyalty. Be part of a vibrant luxury brand redefining the cruise experience!

jobs.title: Vice President Global SPA and Wellness

titleByLocale.it: Vice President Global SPA and Wellness
titleByLocale.en: Vice President Global SPA and Wellness
titleByLocale.de: Vice President Global SPA and Wellness
titleByLocale.fr: Vice President Global SPA and Wellness

jobs.description:
Your Purpose

The Vice President Global SPA and Wellness is the global business owner for all onboard SPA, wellness, beauty, and fitness operations across the Cruise Division of MSC Group, spanning MSC Cruises (Contemporary & Premium) and Explora Journeys (Luxury).

The role has full accountability for SPA and treatment programs, fitness and thermal areas, salon services, wellness retail, medical aesthetics (where applicable), and future digital wellness offerings.

This position ensures that luxury wellness standards, service rituals, therapeutic excellence, and holistic wellbeing philosophies are fully embedded—particularly for Explora Journeys—while maintaining scalable, brand-differentiated wellness frameworks across the fleet.

The VP defines the long-term global SPA and wellness vision, drives commercial performance, elevates guest satisfaction, and evolves wellness concepts that differentiate MSC Group’s Cruise Division across contemporary, premium, and luxury segments. 

Your Impact

Strategic & Commercial Leadership

- Define and lead the global SPA and wellness strategy across contemporary, premium, and luxury brands, aligned with differentiated guest profiles and brand positioning.

- Act as business owner for Global SPA and Wellness, with explicit accountability for luxury wellness concepts and standards.

- Own the end-to-end commercial roadmap, including treatment architecture, pricing strategy, promotions, capacity optimisation, and end-to-end guest journey design.

- Lead strategic negotiations with SPA operators, wellness partners, medical aesthetics providers (where permitted), product brands, and equipment suppliers.

- Drive growth in high-margin and premium wellness categories, including signature treatments, medical aesthetics, fitness and longevity programs, wellness retail, and personalised wellbeing journeys.

- Develop scalable yet brand-distinct SPA and wellness frameworks, enabling selective deployment of premium and luxury concepts across MSC Cruises and Explora Journeys.

Operational and Brand Excellence

- Oversee SPA operations fleet-wide, ensuring consistent service delivery, treatment quality, hygiene standards, and guest satisfaction.

- Define service standards, therapist certification requirements, training programs, and performance benchmarks across all SPA formats.

- Ensure luxury wellness excellence for Explora Journeys, including service rituals, therapist conduct, holistic programming, privacy standards, and immersive wellness environments.

- Introduce tier-specific wellness concepts, signature and luxury rituals, experiential programming, and destination-inspired treatments.

- Ensure fleet readiness for newbuilds and dry docks, including SPA layout design, equipment selection, CAPEX planning, and concept activation.

Financial Management

- Own the full P&L for SPA and Wellness across all Cruise Division brands, including annual budgets, revenue targets, margin improvement, and cost control.

- Optimise therapist productivity, treatment mix, pricing discipline, retail attachment rates, staffing models, and concession economics.

- Leverage advanced analytics to drive demand forecasting, yield management, guest segmentation, booking optimisation, and treatment performance.

Cross-Functional Collaboration

- Partner closely with Hotel Operations, Medical, Fitness, Retail, F&B, Shorex, Digital, Finance, and Newbuild teams to integrate wellness into the overall guest experience.

- Collaborate with CRM and Digital teams on pre-cruise wellness engagement, onboard booking optimisation, and personalised wellness journeys.

- Align SPA offerings with itinerary, destination, and onboard programming to maximise relevance differentiation, and revenue.

Leadership & Organisational Development

- Lead a global HQ and fleet SPA and wellness organisation, including strategy, operations, training, and partner management teams.

- Build a culture of guest-centric service excellence, luxury wellness stewardship, commercial discipline, and continuous innovation.

- Mentor senior leaders and strengthen capabilities across SPA operations, luxury wellness concept development, and data-driven performance management.

Your Journey so far

- 15+ years senior leadership experience in SPA, wellness, hospitality, cruise, or multi-site service businesses.

- Proven success owning complex P&Ls and driving profitability in service-led environments.

- Deep expertise in SPA operations, wellness programming, service quality management, and luxury guest experience standards.

- Strong experience managing third-party operators, concession models, or global vendor partnerships.

Your Essentials

- Excellent leadership, negotiation, and cross-functional stakeholder management skills.

- Willingness to travel extensively and operate in a fast-paced international environment.

Our commitment

We are committed to building a future that values diverse perspectives, embraces the world beyond borders, and fosters an inclusive environment where every individual feels valued, respected and empowered to be their authentic selves. Our commitment extends to taking meaningful, measurable actions that have a long-term positive impact on our guests, our employees and our planet.

Ready to turn your passion into something extraordinary? Join us at MSC Cruises, where new opportunities await. Apply today to be part of a global team that is pushing boundaries and achieving something remarkable. Your journey starts here!

jobs.title: Mobile App Product Specialist
jobs.description:
Your Purpose

We are seeking a dynamic and highly motivated Mobile App Product Specialist to join our Business Innovation team at MSC Cruises Headquarters in Geneva. This role is designed for a data-driven professional with a strong commercial acumen, an analytical mindset, and a passion for optimizing mobile user journeys. The successful candidate will explore in-app behaviors, remove friction in the sales funnel, and drive product-led initiatives to maximize conversion rates and shape the future of digital guest experiences across the MSC for Me digital ecosystem.

Your Impact

- Analyze the MSC for Me in-app user journey and behavior to identify bottlenecks, proposing and validating product initiatives that optimize the sales funnel and boost in-app conversion rates.

- Collaborate with Product, Tech, and UX/UI cross-functional teams to design and implement features that drive organic user acquisition and increase cross-selling/up-selling opportunities for the MSC for Me digital ecosystem.

- Implement and optimize the in-app position of commercial offerings (working closing with Onboard Revenues Commercial Department) to maximize visibility and sales conversions specifically for mobile users.

- Use advanced product analytics tools and data visualization (Power BI) to track sales performance, user activation rates, and drop-offs into the MSC for Me digital ecosystem.

- Act as a bridge between the Onboard Revenues Commercial department and the Tech/Development teams, translating revenue goals into clear digital product requirements to enhance the MSC for Me digital ecosystem.

- Drive MSC for Me  channel-led acquisition by refining native in-app features—such as frictionless guest-to-registered user flows, invite-a-friend mechanisms, and seamless app sharing—to grow the user base.

- Conduct A/B testing on MSC for Me  in-app sales flows, checkout processes, and subscription models to continuously improve operational efficiency and revenue generation.

Your Journey so far

- Bachelor’s or master’s degree in Business Administration, Economics, Marketing Management, Digital or a related field.

- Strong commercial and analytical skills with 3 to 5 years of proven experience in digital sales, product growth, or digital business development, with a strict focus on mobile applications.

- Hands-on experience with app analytics, Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO), and data manipulation/visualization tools (Power BI, SQL; Python is a plus).

- Familiarity with agile methodologies and ability to work side-by-side with developers and product managers in dynamic, fast-paced environments.

- Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to translate data-driven insights into actionable business and product recommendations.

- Creative thinker with a proactive approach to problem-solving, capable of driving "Product-Led Growth" rather than relying on traditional marketing spend.

- Fluent in English; additional European languages are a plus.

Your Essentials

- Deep understanding of mobile user behavior, digital sales funnels, and product-centric acquisition ecosystems.

- Strong business acumen with a focus on maximizing Customer Lifetime Value (CLV) and removing friction from the mobile buying experience.

- Ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver high-quality outputs under tight deadlines.

 

VISA REQUIREMENTS (if any) : 

- Right to work in Switzerland.

Our commitment

We are committed to building a future that values diverse perspectives, embraces the world beyond borders, and fosters an inclusive environment where every individual feels valued, respected and empowered to be their authentic selves. Our commitment extends to taking meaningful, measurable actions that have a long-term positive impact on our guests, our employees and our planet.

Ready to turn your passion into something extraordinary? Join us at MSC Cruises, where new opportunities await. Apply today to be part of a global team that is pushing boundaries and achieving something remarkable. Your journey starts here!

jobs.title: Finance and Accounting Manager - New Investments

titleByLocale.it: Finanza and Accounting Manager - New Investments
titleByLocale.en: Finance and Accounting Manager - New Investments
titleByLocale.de: Finanzen and Accounting Manager - New Investments
titleByLocale.fr: Finance and Accounting Manager - New Investments

jobs.description:
Your Purpose

The Finance and Accounting Manager – New Investments will lead financial oversight, accounting, and performance management for the company’s new Strategic Value Chain Investments (“SVCI”). This role plays a critical part in ensuring strong financial controls, and partnering with cross-functional teams to optimize profitability across non-core cruise operations.
The ideal candidate brings a blend of technical accounting expertise, commercial finance acumen, and experience supporting investment-driven or multi-business environments.

Your Impact

Financial Management & Reporting

- Oversee accounting, month-end close, and financial reporting for SVCI

- Ensure accurate revenue recognition, cost allocation, and margin analysis for SVCI

- Prepare and review management reports, KPIs, and variance analyses to support business decisions

- Maintain compliance with internal controls, accounting standards, and company policies

 

New Investments & Business Evaluation

- Track post-investment performance versus plan and identify risks and improvement opportunities

 

Budgeting & Forecasting

- Assist the investments and the group during budgeting and rolling forecast processes 

- Partner with operational leaders to develop realistic assumptions and financial targets

- Monitor performance against budgets and provide actionable insights

 

Cross-Functional Support

- Act as a finance business partner to support potential synergies within the investments. 

- Collaborate with Legal, Treasury, Operations, and Commercial teams on contract structures, pricing models, and financial implications

- Support strategic initiatives related to expansion, innovation, and optimization of new investments

 

Process Improvement & Systems

- Improve financial processes, reporting automation, and data quality across investment activities

- Support implementation or enhancement of ERP, reporting tools, or financial systems as needed

- Identify opportunities to scale finance processes as new businesses grow

Your Journey so far

- Master’s degree in Finance, Accounting, or related field (CPA, ACA, ACCA, or equivalent strongly preferred) with a Big 4 background

- 6–10 years of progressive experience in finance and accounting, including exposure to investments, or multi-entity environments

- Experience in hospitality, travel, cruise, retail, consumer services, or asset-heavy industries is a strong plus

- Solid understanding of financial modeling, budgeting, forecasting, and management reporting

- Strong knowledge of IFRS accounting principles and financial controls

- Fluent in English, French and/or Italian are a plus

Your Essentials

- Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with a commercial mindset

- Ability to translate financial data into clear insights for non-finance stakeholders

- Comfortable working in a fast-paced, evolving environment with multiple stakeholders

- High attention to detail with the ability to see the big picture

- Excellent communication and relationship-building skills

Our commitment

We are committed to building a future that values diverse perspectives, embraces the world beyond borders, and fosters an inclusive environment where every individual feels valued, respected and empowered to be their authentic selves. Our commitment extends to taking meaningful, measurable actions that have a long-term positive impact on our guests, our employees and our planet.

Ready to turn your passion into something extraordinary? Join us at MSC Cruises, where new opportunities await. Apply today to be part of a global team that is pushing boundaries and achieving something remarkable. Your journey starts here!

jobs.title: Junior Architect Port Development Intern
jobs.description:
Your Purpose

The Junior Architect supports the Port Development team through fast and accurate production of conceptual and technical drawings.
The role is focused on internal execution: transforming design inputs into conceptual documentation and effective visualizations to support decision-making across private destinations, cruise terminals, port infrastructure and related facilities.

Your Impact

- Support the translation of business and design inputs into zoning studies, basic spatial diagrams, and architectural layouts.

- Assist in the development of preliminary layouts following internal design guidance and operational requirements.

- Prepare early stage conceptual design packages for internal review and coordination.

- Produce presentation decks, diagrams, visual narratives, and material boards to support internal communication.

- Develop mandatory 3D models and real time visualizations to support internal reviews and executive level presentations.

- Rapidly update, correct, and revise architectural drawings, including plans, sections, elevations, layouts, façade studies, and circulation diagrams.

- Adapt and coordinate port and civil drawings, including site layouts, infrastructure interfaces, circulation roads, and hardscape coordination.

Your Journey so far

- Strong passion for architecture and the world of interiors, materials and spatial composition.

- Curious and design-oriented, with attention to proportion, atmosphere and detail.

- Execution-driven and highly operational.

- Fast in drawing production and revisions.

- Detail-oriented and structured.

- Comfortable working under tight timelines in a dynamic development environment.

Your Essentials

- Advanced proficiency in AutoCAD with strong drafting speed and precision.

- Strong 3D modeling capabilities in SketchUp or Revit (SketchUp preferred).

- Mandatory experience with real-time rendering tools (Enscape, Twinmotion or equivalent).

- Good knowledge of Photoshop for post-production and corrections.

- Basic knowledge of (or interest in) AI tools such as Stable Diffusion or Magnific AI.

- The ability to independently produce clear, standard-quality visualizations for internal decision-making.

- Portfolio including technical drawings and rendered visualizations to be shared with the CV.

The Junior Architect Port Development Intern must be enrolled in a study program requiring a valid internship agreement. Candidates must hold an EU passport or have the legal right to work in Switzerland. Full professional proficiency in English is required; additional languages are an advantage.

Our commitment

We are committed to building a future that values diverse perspectives, embraces the world beyond borders, and fosters an inclusive environment where every individual feels valued, respected and empowered to be their authentic selves. Our commitment extends to taking meaningful, measurable actions that have a long-term positive impact on our guests, our employees and our planet.
